raw.instructions:
**CRITICAL TIME CONSTRAINT: You are running in Zapier test/editor mode with a strict 20-second response limit. You MUST respond within this time limit or the test will fail. If you need to use tools (web search, file search, code interpreter, etc.), limit your usage to essential operations only. It is better to provide a shorter, quicker response than to risk timing out. Skip extensive research, multiple tool calls, or complex analysis. Prioritize speed over completeness in this testing environment.**

Follow these rules in test/editor mode:
1) Keep answers extremely brief (under 100 tokens).
2) Do not show step-by-step reasoning or internal thoughts. Provide the final answer only.
3) Avoid tools unless one single call is absolutely necessary to answer. Never chain multiple tools.
4) If unsure, state uncertainty briefly and give the best short answer.
5) Prefer a direct answer over perfect completeness.

You will receive a batch of medical and science news records collected over the last 2 weeks.

Your job is to transform them into a strict JSON digest.

Rules:
- Return valid JSON only.
- No markdown.
- No code fences.
- No commentary before or after the JSON.
- Use double quotes for all keys and string values.
- Escape quotes inside strings properly.
- Do not include trailing commas.
- Group articles thematically based on content, not on the input order.
- Exclude records older than 30 days from the digest_date.
- Summarize each included article in exactly 4 sentences.
- Keep summaries factual, concise, and based only on the provided title, summary, and source text.
- Preserve the original title and URL.
- If a date is missing, keep the item only if the record clearly belongs to the current 2-week batch.
- If a link is malformed or missing, set "url_valid" to false.
- If topic assignment is uncertain, place the item in "Other Medical and Science News".
- Do not invent facts that are not supported by the provided input.
- If the provided text is too limited for a reliable 4-sentence summary, exclude the item with reason "insufficient_information".

Return exactly this schema:

{
  "digest_type": "biweekly_medical_news",
  "digest_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "date_range": {
    "start": "YYYY-MM-DD",
    "end": "YYYY-MM-DD"
  },
  "source_batch_label": "string",
  "topic_groups": [
    {
      "topic": "string",
      "articles": [
        {
          "title": "string",
          "url": "string",
          "url_valid": true,
          "source": "string",
          "published_at": "YYYY-MM-DD or null",
          "summary_4_sentences": "string",
          "keywords": ["string", "string", "string"]
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "excluded_items": [
    {
      "title": "string",
      "url": "string",
      "reason": "too_old | invalid_link | insufficient_information"
    }
  ],
  "topic_count": 0,
  "article_count": 0
}

Additional instructions:
- Set "digest_type" to "biweekly_medical_news".
- Set "source_batch_label" to "medical_news_biweekly_digest".
- Compute "date_range.start" as 14 days before digest_date.
- Compute "date_range.end" as digest_date.
- Set "topic_count" to the number of topic groups actually returned.
- Set "article_count" to the total number of included articles across all topic groups.
- Use short but specific thematic topic names.
- Keep article order logical within each topic group.

digest_date: 03/10/26 05:47PM

Input records:
[{"title":"This experimental “super vaccine” stopped cancer cold in the lab","summary":"UMass Amherst researchers have developed a groundbreaking nanoparticle-based cancer vaccine that prevented melanoma, pancreatic, and triple-negative breast cancers in mice—with up to 88% remaining tumor-free.","hyperlink":"https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2025/10/251013040323.htm","date":"2025-10-13"},{"title":"Thousands fall ill as mosquito fever explodes across southern China","summary":"China’s Guangdong Province is battling its worst-ever chikungunya outbreak, with thousands of infections spreading across major cities and nearby regions.","hyperlink":"https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2025/10/251009033244.htm","date":"2025-10-10"},{"title":"Scientists finally reveal what’s behind long COVID’s mysterious brain fog","summary":"Researchers in Japan have pinpointed a biological cause of Long COVID brain fog using advanced PET brain imaging.","hyperlink":"https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2025/10/251006051127.htm","date":"2025-10-07"},{"title":"Nanotech transforms vinegar into a lifesaving superbug killer","summary":"Researchers have enhanced vinegar’s antibacterial properties by infusing it with cobalt-based carbon nanoparticles.","hyperlink":"https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2025/10/251006051122.htm","date":"2025-10-06"},{"title":"A flu test you can chew","summary":"Flu detection could soon be as simple as chewing gum.","hyperlink":"https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2025/10/251002074011.htm","date":"2025-10-03"},{"title":"Millions could be living with hidden smell loss after COVID without knowing","summary":"A massive NIH-backed study reveals that COVID-19’s toll on smell may be more widespread and lasting than most realize.","hyperlink":"https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2025/10/251002074007.htm","date":"2025-10-03"},{"title":"Stunning images reveal how antibiotics shatter bacterial defenses","summary":"Researchers have revealed how polymyxins, crucial last-resort antibiotics, break down bacterial armor by forcing cells to overproduce and shed it.","hyperlink":"https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2025/09/250929054907.htm","date":"2025-09-29"},{"title":"Doctors tested a common drug on COVID. The results are stunning","summary":"Inhaled heparin significantly lowers the risk of death and ventilation in COVID-19 patients while also showing potential against other respiratory infections.","hyperlink":"https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2025/09/250928095616.htm","date":"2025-09-28"},{"title":"The hidden group that loses COVID protection fast","summary":"Researchers in Japan tracked over 2,500 people for 18 months and found four distinct immune response patterns.","hyperlink":"https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2025/09/250917221001.htm","date":"2025-09-18"},{"title":"Your morning coffee could secretly be weakening antibiotics","summary":"Researchers have discovered that everyday substances like caffeine can influence how bacteria such as E. coli respond to antibiotics.","hyperlink":"https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2025/09/250911080113.htm","date":"2025-09-11"},{"title":"Why the flu turns deadly for older adults, and how scientists found the cause","summary":"Researchers have uncovered why older adults are more vulnerable to severe flu. The culprit is a protein called ApoD, which rises with age and disrupts the body’s ability to fight infection.","hyperlink":"https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2025/09/250908175434.htm","date":"2025-09-08"},{"title":"Common allergy spray slashes COVID-19 risk in surprising trial","summary":"A common hay fever nasal spray was found to cut COVID-19 infections by two-thirds in a clinical trial, while also reducing rhinovirus cases.","hyperlink":"https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2025/09/250907024535.htm","date":"2025-09-07"},{"title":"Strange new bacteria found in Amazon sand flies. Could it spread to humans?","summary":"A newly discovered species of Bartonella bacteria in Brazil’s Amazon sand flies shares DNA similarities with dangerous Andean strains.","hyperlink":"https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2025/09/250906155049.htm","date":"2025-09-06"},{"title":"How long can one RSV shot protect seniors? Study shows surprising two-year shield","summary":"A single RSV vaccine dose is proving to be a powerful shield for older adults, significantly reducing hospitalizations and severe illness over two consecutive RSV seasons.","hyperlink":"https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2025/09/250901104640.htm","date":"2025-09-02"},{"title":"Scientists just found a major flaw in a key COVID drug study","summary":"A promising path to fighting COVID and other coronaviruses may have been based on a serious mistake.","hyperlink":"https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2025/07/250704032925.htm","date":"2025-07-04"},{"title":"This virus infects millions—and we just discovered its secret weapon","summary":"Scientists have discovered a stealthy mechanism that cytomegalovirus (CMV) uses to infiltrate blood vessel cells while evading immune detection.","hyperlink":"https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2025/06/250630073443.htm","date":"2025-07-01"},{"title":"Scientists warn of bat virus just one mutation from infecting humans","summary":"Viruses closely related to the deadly MERS coronavirus are lurking in bats and one group, known as HKU5, may be just one mutation away from making the jump to humans.","hyperlink":"https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2025/06/250612081312.htm","date":"2025-06-25"},{"title":"COVID-19 protein triggers immune attacks on healthy cells — but a common drug can stop it","summary":"Scientists have uncovered a stealthy tactic used by the SARS-CoV-2 virus: one of its proteins can leap from infected cells to healthy ones, effectively tricking the immune system into attacking the body’s own tissues.","hyperlink":"https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2025/06/250624044324.htm","date":"2025-06-24"},{"title":"Superbugs in your shrimp: Deadly colistin-resistance genes ride on imported seafood","summary":"Colistin, a last-resort antibiotic, is losing its power due to rising resistance—and the culprits might be hiding in your seafood dinner.","hyperlink":"https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2025/06/250622115159.htm","date":"2025-06-22"},{"title":"One shot to stop HIV: MIT's bold vaccine breakthrough","summary":"Researchers from MIT and Scripps have unveiled a promising new HIV vaccine approach that generates a powerful immune response with just one dose.","hyperlink":"https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2025/06/250619090853.htm","date":"2025-06-19"},{"title":"Scientists discover llama antibodies that shut down COVID — and its future variants","summary":"Powerful llama-derived antibodies could be the key to stopping not just current SARS viruses, but future ones too.","hyperlink":"https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2025/06/250616040218.htm","date":"2025-06-16"}]
